$comment: Lifecycle hooks for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex runtimes.
These fire on the host IDE's native hook points and delegate to
the opus-mind Python helpers. No LLM synthesis happens here —
hooks only EMIT audit data or block a tool call when a gate
Every hook is a Bash one-liner that shells out to the scripts/
directory. If opus-mind is installed via install-skill.sh, the
The host runtime decides what to do with a non-zero exit:
- Claude Code: PreToolUse non-zero blocks the tool call.
- Cursor:      errorOccurred non-zero surfaces in the sidebar.
- Codex:       request_exec non-zero aborts the session step.

PreToolUse.command: python3 $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/audit.py --json "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f"[opus-mind pre-edit] {d[\"structural_health\"]} ({d[\"verdict\"]})", file=sys.stderr)'
PreToolUse.note: Informs Claude of current score before it edits; never blocks.

PostToolUse.command: python3 $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/audit.py --json "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f"[opus-mind post-edit] {d[\"structural_health\"]} ({d[\"verdict\"]})", file=sys.stderr)'
PostToolUse.note: Shows score delta after each edit so the user sees whether the change helped.

Stop.note: Informational — runs self-audit on session exit, stores nothing.

errorOccurred.note: When Cursor surfaces a prompt-level failure, try matching it to a known primitive pointer. Fails gracefully.

CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT.description: Set automatically by Claude Code; points at the installed skill directory. Used by every hook above.

OPUS_MIND_ROOT.description: Optional override for Cursor / Codex. Defaults to the Claude Code path.
